reason: Response B has the punchier, shorter-sentence rhythm and tight comedic escalation characteristic of Haiku, with a clean conceptual through-line (quantum superposition, migration locations, the "hospital effect") and a crisp callback ending. Response A leans on a single overextended "sentient pen conspiracy" metaphor with more padded, run-on phrasing that reads like a larger model imitating a quirky voice. B's economy of language and rapid-fire structure better matches genuine Haiku output.
